text.title: Regression Test First Bugfix Workflow — Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from alert to merged fix with red-test-first discipline.

list.item: Input: Sentry/Datadog/ticket URL.
Action: copy stack trace, identify reproducing inputs (request body, env, locale, time).
Output: `trigger.url` + reproducible-input dictionary recorded in the workflow record.
Decision gate: if inputs not deterministic, route to "non-deterministic" branch and skip this methodology.
Input: reproducing inputs from Step 1.
Action: minimal repro in the dev environment. Confirm the same exception / wrong output.
Output: local repro script or REPL transcript.

section.title: Step 3 — Author the red test FIRST

list.item: Input: local repro.

Action: write a failing test that encodes "this must never happen again" — at minimum status code, side-effect absence, and any observable state. Commit it on the fix branch BEFORE writing any production code. The commit message contains the alert URL.
Output: failing test at `tests/regression/test_<short-slug>.py`, committed with hash recorded in `red_test.commit`.
Input: red test + root-cause analysis.
Action: smallest possible change that flips the red test to green. Refactor only if directly tied to the root cause; otherwise file a follow-up.
Output: production-code diff; red test now passes locally.
Input: PR with red test + fix.
Action: push, run CI, capture run URL.
Output: `verification.ci_run_url` and `passed: true`.
Decision gate: if CI is flaky on unrelated tests, isolate the relevant job; do not merge with `passed: false`.
Input: green PR.
Action: merge with squash. Set `review.next_review_at` (default = today + 90 days). On the next review, confirm the alert has not resurfaced.
Output: closed loop; deprecate the workflow record only after one full review window with zero recurrence.
